title: Conveyor Won't Start
briefing: The conveyor belt on Line 1 won't start. The operator hit the start button three times and nothing happened. The motor control centre shows no faults. Production is stopped. Find the fault and get it running.

tags.label: START_BTN
tags.description: Momentary start pushbutton — normally open, closes when pressed

tags.label: ESTOP_NC
tags.description: Emergency stop — normally closed contact. TRUE = circuit intact, FALSE = E-stop pressed or wiring fault

tags.label: OVERLOAD_RESET
tags.description: Motor overload thermal relay — normally closed. Opens on overload trip.

tags.label: MOTOR_SEAL
tags.description: Memory bit — seals the start circuit so motor keeps running after start button is released

tags.label: M1_RUN
tags.description: Motor 1 run output — energises the motor starter contactor

tags.label: RUN_INDICATOR
tags.description: Panel indicator lamp — illuminates when motor is running

rungs.description: Start latch: (START OR SEAL) AND ESTOP AND OVERLOAD → motor run
rungs.condition: (I0.0 OR M0.0) AND I0.1 AND I0.2

rungs.description: Seal bit: copies motor run output to seal memory
Run indicator lamp mirrors motor run

faults.effect: E-stop wired as normally-open instead of normally-closed. The input reads FALSE (open) even when the E-stop is not pressed — breaking the start circuit.
clues: I0.1 reads 0 on the PLC despite E-stop being out (not pressed)
Multimeter on E-stop terminals shows 0V with E-stop out — should be 24V
Wiring diagram shows I0.1 should be connected to NC contact, but field wiring is on NO terminal
faults.solution: Move the field wire from the NO terminal to the NC terminal on the E-stop device at junction box JB-1.

redHerrings.description: The overload reset button on the MCC has a faded label and looks like it might have tripped
redHerrings.whyMisleading: OVERLOAD_RESET (I0.2) reads TRUE — the overload relay is healthy. The label is just old. Pressing it does nothing.

derekScript.initialCall: Yeah it's Derek. Line 1 conveyor is down, we've got a full pallet backed up. I need this running in the next ten minutes or we're pulling from Line 2. What's the situation?
derekScript.checkIn5min: You still on it? It's been five minutes. I've got the shift supervisor breathing down my neck.
derekScript.impatient10min: Ten minutes. This is taking way too long. Did you check the E-stop circuit? Sometimes the sparky wires it wrong.
derekScript.angry15min: Fifteen minutes of downtime. That's coming out of someone's report. I'm coming down there.
derekScript.resolution: Alright, belt's moving. Write it up in the maintenance log — NC/NO swap on E-stop wiring. I'll have a word with whoever commissioned this panel.
